scenarios.description: Run two Orchard tenants through the production SignalR Redis backplane configuration and prove tenant-qualified agent, queue, supervisor, reconnect, and fan-out delivery.
blockedBy: The current transport harness uses tenant-scoped hosts and production backplane registration but does not activate features through a complete Orchard tenant setup.
R8 must extend the minimal provider-listener harness to authenticated ContactCenterHub agent, queue, supervisor, reconnect, and fan-out scenarios.
Retained release evidence has not yet been produced for every candidate GA topology.
harnessDependencies: Two configured Orchard shells with distinct tenant identities
Authenticated agent and supervisor SignalR clients

scenarios.description: Run two application processes against one tenant database while both receive duplicate and reordered provider events, then prove one monotonic provider transition.
blockedBy: The repository has no two-process Orchard Contact Center harness.
Provider inbox uniqueness, canonical provider identity, and sequence high-water state do not exist.
harnessDependencies: Two independently hosted Orchard application processes
Provider inbox and call-session state inspector

scenarios.description: Expire or revoke the active provider-listener lease while events are flowing and prove ownership transfers without a delivery gap, split brain, or state regression.
blockedBy: Provider stream ownership has no durable renewable lease or fencing token.
No multi-process provider-listener harness can pause, expire, and transfer ownership deterministically.
harnessDependencies: Controllable lease clock and fencing store

scenarios.description: Terminate one application process during offers, provider commands, outbox dispatch, and active calls, then prove surviving nodes recover without duplicate customer actions or capacity leaks.
blockedBy: Canonical agent availability, durable provider commands, and stable outbox handler ids are not implemented.
The repository has no process-kill orchestration or surviving-node convergence probe.
harnessDependencies: Two independently hosted Orchard application processes

scenarios.description: Partition one application process from Redis while database and provider connectivity remain available, then prove readiness degradation, reconnect convergence, and tenant-safe real-time behavior.
blockedBy: No Contact Center Redis readiness model exists.
No network-fault proxy is wired into a multi-process Contact Center harness.
harnessDependencies: Two independently hosted Orchard application processes
Redis backplane behind a controllable network-fault proxy

scenarios.description: Partition one or all application processes from the tenant database during routing, reservation, provider ingest, and outbox work, then prove fail-closed behavior and bounded recovery.
blockedBy: Database readiness and degraded-mode behavior are not defined.
The distributed harness has no network-fault proxy or database recovery oracle.
harnessDependencies: Two independently hosted Orchard application processes
PostgreSQL behind a controllable network-fault proxy
Reservation, inbox, and outbox convergence assertions

scenarios.description: Run N and N-1 application processes concurrently while replaying pending outbox, provider, and active-work state, then prove stable handler ids and expand-migrate-contract compatibility.
blockedBy: Stable versioned outbox handler ids are not implemented.
No N/N-1 package fixture or expand-migrate-contract deployment harness exists.
harnessDependencies: Version-pinned N and N-1 Orchard application images
Schema and handler replay compatibility assertions
